content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (ICM/RND) from autonomous agent research can be repurposed to dynamically prune and prioritize memory storage in cutting-edge 2024-2025 active learning agents, resolving the key bottleneck of exploding memory footprint that plagues state-of-the-art open-ended active agents. This framework scores every new stored memory based on how much it reduces the agent's prediction error (the core metric of curiosity mechanisms), only retaining memories that improve future active learning performance.
